data.content:
Implementation plan

Product/UI shape
- Replace the shipped right-side `StaleBeatGroomingPanel` rail with a first-class stale-beats cue in the overview tab strip area. The cue should behave like a special overview action/tab: show stale count, show queued/running count when reviews are in flight, and open a dialog/lightbox instead of consuming fixed right-column width. This directly fixes the built-in browser clipping at `src/components/beat-state-overview.tsx:209`.
- Build a new dialog component, likely `src/components/stale-beat-grooming-dialog.tsx`, and keep `src/components/beat-state-overview.tsx` responsible only for overview tab/matrix integration. Remove the `lg:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_320px]` rail layout.
- In the dialog, order stale beats by age since `updated` descending, not `created`, because the follow-up clarified “beats that have not been updated for the longest period of time.” Keep the 7-day cutoff and the helper injectable by clock for tests. Display both updated-age and created-age only if it remains compact; primary sort/age is last updated.
- Replace the freeform model textbox with a configured model/agent select. It should default to the dispatch-selected stale-grooming agent/model and include every configured registered agent as an override option. Label the control as model/agent clearly enough that users understand these are configured runnable models, not arbitrary model ids.
- Add bulk controls in the dialog: `Select all`, `Oldest five`, and `Clear selection` or equivalent. “Oldest five” is the first-pass wording for “knock off a few”: it selects a small, useful batch without making the user manually uncheck many rows. The Review button should reflect the selected count.
- Preserve row click-through to beat detail and keep review results advisory only. No automatic beat closing, scope mutation, or workflow state changes.

Dispatch/settings design
- Add stale grooming to the settings contract next to scope refinement:
  - `src/lib/types.ts`: extend `ActionName` with `staleGrooming`.
  - `src/lib/schemas.ts`: add `actions.staleGrooming` and `pools.stale_grooming`; update descriptions and default settings.
  - `src/lib/settings-types.ts`, `src/lib/settings-update.ts`, `src/lib/agent-pool.ts`, and `src/lib/settings-agent-removal.ts`: include stale grooming in action usage/swap/removal accounting.
  - `src/lib/settings-dispatch-targets.ts`: add a shared dispatch pool target for `stale_grooming`, parallel to `scope_refinement`.
  - `src/components/settings-actions-section.tsx` and `src/components/settings-pools-section.tsx`: expose Stale Grooming under basic action mappings and advanced weighted pools.
- Replace `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-agent.ts` direct selected-agent-only resolver with a resolver that supports two modes:
  1. Default: advanced `pools.stale_grooming` first, then basic `actions.staleGrooming`.
  2. Explicit override: any configured registered agent id.
  Missing config must fail loudly with `FOOLERY GROOMING FAILURE`; no first-agent fallback.
- Add an API/options helper that exposes all configured override choices plus the current default selected agent id for the UI.

First-class API design
- Keep compatibility with current `GET/POST /api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews`, but make the API complete for external agents such as OpenClaw/Hermes. Proposed routes:
  - `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming`: list stale beats for `_repo` or active/all repo context, sorted oldest-updated first; support `limit`, `ageDays`, and possibly `includeReviews=1`.
  - `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming/options`: list configured agents/models and the dispatch default.
  - `POST /api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews`: enqueue explicit `targets`, or `{ mode: "oldest", limit: 5 }` for the oldest N. Accept optional `agentId` override; omit it to use dispatch default.
  - `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews`: list review records, filterable by status/beat/repo if inexpensive.
  - `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming/status`: return queue size, worker health, active jobs, recent failures, recent completions/reviews, and stale count.
- Add these paths to `src/lib/openapi-spec.ts` via a new `src/lib/openapi/paths-stale-grooming.ts`, and update `docs/API.md` or API README so external agents have a stable contract.

Worker/diagnostics design
- Bring stale grooming worker telemetry up to the scope-refinement standard rather than relying only on the review store. Add `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-worker-state.ts` or equivalent with worker count/running flag, active jobs, total completed/failed, recent failures, and uptime.
- Add enqueue listeners to `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-queue.ts` so worker startup/status behaves more like scope refinement. Keep processing simple unless concurrency is needed; health shape can support one worker initially.
- Add `src/components/stale-beat-grooming-diagnostics-card.tsx` and render it in `src/components/runtime-diagnostics-panel.tsx` beside `ScopeRefinementDiagnosticsCard`.

Test strategy
- Expand `src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming.test.ts` for updated-age cutoff/sort descending, oldest-N target selection, select-all/oldest-five payload shaping, and repo-qualified keys.
- Expand route tests for listing stale beats, default dispatch resolution, explicit override, oldest-five enqueue, missing stale-grooming config loud failure, and status payload.
- Add settings/schema tests for `staleGrooming` action and `stale_grooming` pool defaults/removal impact.
- Add diagnostics/status endpoint tests modeled on `scope-refinement-status-endpoint.test.ts`.
- Run full gates: `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in the implementation worktree, `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build`, and built-in browser review of overview and diagnostics.

Planned child slices
- foolery-2ee2: dispatch/settings/API work.
- foolery-79f2: overview stale grooming dialog and selection UX.
- foolery-d57e: diagnostics card/status worker health.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: large but well bounded. The API/settings work is the riskiest because it touches shared settings schema, removal accounting, and OpenAPI/docs.
- Main UX risk: model-vs-agent language. The implementation should phrase the select as configured models while still storing the selected registered agent id, because in this codebase the runnable model is represented by an agent config entry.
- Main behavioral risk: dispatch pools are weighted, so “default selected model” needs a stable UI interpretation. Recommended first pass: expose a concrete dispatch default selected by the same resolver when options are fetched, then treat user selection as an explicit override in the review request.
- Preserve the advisory-only invariant throughout: AI output may recommend still-do/reshape/drop, but does not change the beat automatically.
